title: test: Fix expected output for pause/unpause with runc
body:
Fix issues with `080-pause` test with runc.  When run as rootless, runc issues an extra warning on `podman pause` & `podman unpause`:

```
$ podman pause sleeper
WARN[0000] runc pause may fail if you don't have the full access to cgroups 
sleeper
$ podman unpause sleeper
WARN[0000] runc resume may fail if you don't have the full access to cgroups 
sleeper
```

OS: openSUSE Tumbleweed with podman 5.4.1 & runc 1.2.5

Example messages:

```
# [04:17:56.327901988] $ /usr/bin/podman --noout pause 765a406122b7d902034f6511412cb0e3da38f299bb1dac91bf481877c2ab9c2c
# [04:17:56.386516060] time="2025-04-03T04:17:56-04:00" level=warning msg="runc pause may fail if you don't have the full access to cgroups"
# #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
# #|     FAIL: output should be empty
# #| expected: '[no output]'
# #|   actual: 'time="2025-04-03T04:17:56-04:00" level=warning msg="runc pause may fail if you don't have the full access to cgroups"'
```

```
# [04:17:57.417230523] $ /usr/bin/podman unpause --all
# [04:17:57.484672847] time="2025-04-03T04:17:57-04:00" level=warning msg="runc resume may fail if you don't have the full access to cgroups"
# 079d6fb9193a595477eea31e4fb9b621fa33c9da52f4870fb578304b99a71f9b
# #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
# #|     FAIL: podman unpause output
# #| expected: '079d6fb9193a595477eea31e4fb9b621fa33c9da52f4870fb578304b99a71f9b'
# #|   actual: 'time="2025-04-03T04:17:57-04:00" level=warning msg="runc resume may fail if you don't have the full access to cgroups"'
# #|         > '079d6fb9193a595477eea31e4fb9b621fa33c9da52f4870fb578304b99a71f9b'
# #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
```

The only issue now is with healthcheck:

```
not ok 3 [080] podman pause/unpause with HealthCheck interval
# tags: ci:parallel
# (from function `bail-now' in file test/system/helpers.bash, line 187,
#  from function `assert' in file test/system/helpers.bash, line 1086,
#  in test file test/system/080-pause.bats, line 134)
#   `assert "$output" == "" "service should not be running"' failed
#
# [08:25:32.843124916] $ /usr/bin/podman run -d --name c-t3-xdunlb5u --health-cmd echo healthmsg-p1Z9tEV5zn --health-interval 1s quay.io/libpod/testimage:20241011 /home/podman/pause
# [08:25:33.086892068] 3fd538c6e92736f5a9727f0f61f8ef6db00e386641c418bf36cd196a3925d45e
#
# [08:25:33.093273095] $ /usr/bin/podman healthcheck run c-t3-xdunlb5u
#
# [08:25:33.456271990] $ /usr/bin/podman --noout pause c-t3-xdunlb5u
# [08:25:33.529155984] time="2025-04-04T08:25:33-04:00" level=warning msg="runc pause may fail if you don't have the full access to cgroups"
# #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
# #|     FAIL: service should not be running
# #| expected: ''
# #|   actual: ● 3fd538c6e92736f5a9727f0f61f8ef6db00e386641c418bf36cd196a3925d45e-2bc7b28e0a787c3a.timer - \[systemd-run\] /usr/bin/podman healthcheck run 3fd538c6e92736f5a9727f0f61f8ef6db00e386641c418bf36cd196a3925d45e
# #|         >      Loaded: loaded \(/run/user/1000/systemd/transient/3fd538c6e92736f5a9727f0f61f8ef6db00e386641c418bf36cd196a3925d45e-2bc7b28e0a787c3a.timer\; transient\)
# #|         >   Transient: yes
# #|         >      Active: active \(waiting\) since Fri 2025-04-04 08:25:33 EDT\; 494ms ago
# #|         >  Invocation: fc86f4327b55427198b60bc5522f10e4
# #|         >     Trigger: Fri 2025-04-04 08:25:34 EDT\; 729ms left
# #|         >    Triggers: ● 3fd538c6e92736f5a9727f0f61f8ef6db00e386641c418bf36cd196a3925d45e-2bc7b28e0a787c3a.service
# #|         > ''
# #|         > Apr 04 08:25:33 susetest systemd\[1388\]: Started \[systemd-run\] /usr/bin/podman healthcheck run 3fd538c6e92736f5a9727f0f61f8ef6db00e386641c418bf36cd196a3925d45e.
# #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
# # [teardown]

```

I am not sure this is the right thing to do? Why does runc always print a warning? The container should have a proper cgroup setup I assume (from podman at least) so always a logging a warning to end users feels wrong.

Where does the warning come from all of the sudden? AFAICT the runc warning has been added in https://github.com/opencontainers/runc/commit/06f789cf26774dd64cb2a9cc0b3c6a6ff832733b#diff-1d607b3dbeeec2008f38d0684756bf0613fb9c42bbeec17642a64d21d30c9dc6 which was a really long time ago.

As such I rather understand why there is a warning to begin with and not work around it.
cc @kolyshkin

> I am not sure this is the right thing to do? Why does runc always print a warning? The container should have a proper cgroup setup I assume (from podman at least) so always a logging a warning to end users feels wrong.

It may be indeed a bug in runc as the proper delegation is done:

```
$ cat /etc/systemd/system/user@.service.d/60-delegate.conf
[Service]
Delegate=cpu cpuset io memory pids
```

Verification run: https://openqa.opensuse.org/tests/4975720#external

- root / local: https://openqa.opensuse.org/tests/4975720/file/podman_integration-bats-root-local.tap
- root / remote: https://openqa.opensuse.org/tests/4975720/file/podman_integration-bats-root-remote.tap
- rootless / local: https://openqa.opensuse.org/tests/4975720/file/podman_integration-bats-user-local.tap
- rootless / remote: https://openqa.opensuse.org/tests/4975720/file/podman_integration-bats-user-remote.tap

I tried runc-1.0.0 which was released in 2021, and got the same result:

```console
[kir@kir-tp1 runc]$ podman --runtime=$PWD/runc-1.0.0 run -d alpine sleep 1h
0c53f523c7aa9c929bfc19b37c2f50a23e139dac6f5fd60419c54c92d3b96323
[kir@kir-tp1 runc]$ podman pause 0c53f523c7aa9c929bfc19b37c2f50a23e139dac6f5fd60419c54c92d3b96323
WARN[0000] runc pause may fail if you don't have the full access to cgroups 
0c53f523c7aa9c929bfc19b37c2f50a23e139dac6f5fd60419c54c92d3b96323
[kir@kir-tp1 runc]$ podman unpause 0c53f523c7aa9c929bfc19b37c2f50a23e139dac6f5fd60419c54c92d3b96323
WARN[0000] runc resume may fail if you don't have the full access to cgroups 
0c53f523c7aa9c929bfc19b37c2f50a23e139dac6f5fd60419c54c92d3b96323
```

I think we haven't changed anything in this area since 2021.

I did some testing, and it looks like @ricardobranco777 is not using systemd cgroup manager/driver. In this case, runc's shouldUseRootlessCgroupManager uses this code:

https://github.com/opencontainers/runc/blob/c3a41d77db71d9ba8bd76cdcd74d8aa71d16d2a8/rootless_linux.go#L48-L52

which results in this warning.
As for the test case, i think the proper thing to do is to filter out the warning.

> I did some testing, and it looks like @ricardobranco777 is not using systemd cgroup manager/driver. 

```
$ podman info -f '{{ .Host.CgroupManager }}'
systemd
```

> > I did some testing, and it looks like @ricardobranco777 is not using systemd cgroup manager/driver.
> 
> ```
> $ podman info -f '{{ .Host.CgroupManager }}'
> systemd
> ```

Yep, I was wrong.

This probably means that podman do not pass `--systemd-cgroups` parameter to runc when doing pause/unpause. Which is not a problem per se.

In any case, this is what I came up with: https://github.com/opencontainers/runc/pull/4709. If you can test it that'd be awesome.
@giuseppe Do we need to pass `--systemd-cgroups` in podman? Searching in our code I only see that being passed in buildah but not in podman.

> @giuseppe Do we need to pass `--systemd-cgroups` in podman? Searching in our code I only see that being passed in buildah but not in podman.

My expectation is that the OCI runtime stores the handler used for the container and use that, but I don't see anything wrong if we pass `--systemd-cgroup` to the OCI runtime when the container is using systemd.
@ricardobranco777 Looks like the runc PRs are merged, so I guess we can close this here then instead?

> @ricardobranco777 Looks like the runc PRs are merged, so I guess we can close this here then instead?

Yes. Makes no sense to add these ugly workarounds around fixed warnings.  Thanks to everyone involved in fixing this.
